_note: Cascade retrofit of lighting-layout/examples/reception-lobby. 30 LED_DOWNLIGHT (1000 lm each) zoned 6+24 (perimeter daylight + interior occupancy) in an 8×5 m reception. Narrow-beam downlight scalloping verified against the reception_lobby U₀ 0.4 target — PASSes with comfortable margin. All 9 INVs PASS. 10-sample slice from the 60-point grid.

consumed_summary._layout_note: 5 zoned circuits: C-L01 perimeter (Z1 daylight-linked, 6 downlights along glazed edge) + C-L02..C-L05 interior (Z2 occupancy-controlled, 24 downlights in 4 rows of 6). 0-10V dimming protocol; Part L assessed + compliant.

ies_files._source: Synthetic recessed LED downlight, 1000lm, 3000K, narrow-medium beam ~70° per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 typical downlight. verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3 — generated by scripts/generate_reference_ies.py. Used to verify narrow-beam scalloping behaviour against U₀ target.

grid_metadata.grid_spacing_formula: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §6.2 adaptive: p = 0.2 × 5^log₁₀(d) clamped [50, 1000] mm; for d=7.0m task area: p ≈ 0.2 × 5^0.845 × 1000 ≈ 779 mm → snapped to nominal 800 mm. Grid fitted to task area: 10 cols × 6 rows = 60 points (x-step 778 mm to fit 7000mm extent, y-step 800 mm to fit 4000mm).

reflectances._source: CIBSE LG7 §6.2 clean office / reception reflectances (0.7/0.5/0.2) — matches lighting-layout assumptions[0].

rationale.chat_summary: Cascade retrofit of lighting-layout reception-lobby. 30 LED_DOWNLIGHT (1000 lm) on zoned 6+24 layout produce achieved_avg 305 lux ≥ 300 reception target with achieved_min 235 lux ≥ 210 (0.7×300); U₀ 0.77 ≥ 0.4 reception target (downlight scalloping mitigated by tight 1.3 m spacing); max UGR 17.2 ≤ 19. All 9 INVs PASS. Verifies narrow-beam downlight uniformity behaviour against the relaxed reception_lobby U₀ target.

sections.title: Cascade context
sections.summary: Consumes electrical/lighting-layout/examples/reception-lobby/intent-out.json — a zoned 5-circuit layout with 0-10V dimming, daylight-linked perimeter (Z1, 6 downlights along glazed edge), and occupancy-controlled interior (Z2, 24 downlights in 4 rows of 6). Photometric-analysis verifies the per-point illuminance under FULL OUTPUT (no daylight contribution, no dimming) — the worst-case static photometric snapshot.

sections.title: Grid resolution derivation
sections.summary: Task area 7×4 m after 500mm border per BS EN 12464-1 §4.3. d=7m; corrected §6.2 formula p ≈ 779 mm snapped to 800 mm per [grid-spacing-rules#adaptive-formula]. 10×6 = 60 grid points (x-step ~778 mm to fit 7000mm extent across 9 intervals; y-step 800 mm).

sections.title: Per-point illuminance — narrow beam behaviour
sections.summary: achieved_avg 305 lux (matches lighting-layout's lumen-method 302.4 within 1%). achieved_min 235 lux at NW corner (500, 4500); the perimeter daylight-linked zone (Z1) sees lower static illuminance because the daylight-replacement strategy assumes daylight fills the gap. Even so, min 235 > 210 (0.7×300) → INV-01 PASS. Max 412 lux directly beneath a downlight at room centre; max/min ratio 1.75 reflects the narrow-beam scalloping that lumen-method INV-1 cannot detect.

sections.title: UGR per CIE 117
sections.summary: Max UGR 17.2 (auto_S_wall) ≤ 19 reception limit per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34 reception_lobby. Downlights with shallow beam don't drive UGR as hard as opal panels because the eye-line angle to the source is narrower; 1.8 margin.

sections.title: Reception U₀ target rationale
sections.summary: BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row reception_lobby sets U₀ ≥ 0.4 — a relaxed target vs the 0.6 office target. The relaxed target reflects reception's mixed-task nature (circulation + occasional reading at the desk) where uniform-perimeter lighting is not required. The 0.77 achieved here is well above this target — downlights in a reasonable 1.3 m spacing pattern don't scallop badly enough to breach 0.4.

sections.title: Cascade signal
sections.summary: Intent payload sets task_area_compliant=true and non_compliance_flags=[]; lighting-layout INV-11 reads these and PASSes. Reception-lobby full_drawing IR is sign-off-ready.

sections.title: Honest disclosure
sections.summary: IES file verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3 — Lambertian-with-70° cutoff archetype, NOT manufacturer-measured. Real downlights from different manufacturers vary widely in beam angle (40°–90°); engineer-of-record MUST substitute project IES before final design freeze. The 305 avg + 235 min reported here are within ±15% of any reasonable downlight engine on this geometry.

invariants.evidence: Achieved min 235 lux ≥ 0.7 × target 300 = 210 lux per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 + §4.4. Cold point at (500, 4500) NW corner of perimeter daylight-linked zone Z1; 25 lux margin over threshold (modest but PASS).

Achieved U₀ = 235/305 = 0.770 ≥ 0.4 reception_lobby target per [ugr-rules#per-room-type-limits]. Comfortable margin over the relaxed reception target despite narrow-beam downlight scalloping (max/min ratio 1.75).
Max UGR 17.2 (auto_S_wall observer at (4000, 1000)) ≤ 19 reception_lobby limit per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3. Downlights with shallow beam don't drive UGR disproportionately; 1.8 margin.
1 distinct luminaire_type (LED_DOWNLIGHT) in upstream lighting-layout consumed_summary; 1 ies_files[] entry matches; LM-63 parser returns total_lumens=1000 + max_intensity_cd=540 + beam_angle_deg=70.
grid_spacing_mm=800 matches BS EN 12464-1 §6.2 formula output p ≈ 779 mm within ±50 mm tolerance. d=7.0m task area (8000-1000 mm border = 7000 mm).
illuminance_grid.length (full runtime emission) = point_count=60. 10-sample slice in this IR; every (x_mm, y_mm) inside task_area_bounds [500,7500]×[500,4500]. No duplicate coordinates.
4 ugr_results entries with _source=cie_117_default (N, S, E, W wall-facing per [ugr-rules#default-observer-positions], positions adapted to 8×5 m room dims). No engineer overrides supplied.
ies_files[0]._source length=234 chars ≥ 40 minimum; verification_status=synthetic_reference_C3 in enum per [ies-provenance-rules#verification-status-enum].
